input.headline: Cooking Recipes, advice and inspiration for everyday cooking, special occasions and more.

raw.headline: Cooking Recipes, advice and inspiration for everyday cooking, special occasions and more.

winner: Why does my "everyday cooking" recipe book have a chapter titled "Microwave Oven: The Last Resort"? Because after 20 years of trying, I still can't boil an egg without setting off the smoke alarm.

candidates.text: Why does my "everyday cooking" recipe book have a chapter titled "Microwave Oven: The Last Resort"? Because after 20 years of trying, I still can't boil an egg without setting off the smoke alarm.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific with a relatable situation, concise and funny with a clever twist."}

candidates.text: Why did the chef bring a ladder to the kitchen? Because he wanted to reach new heights in his cooking... and also to climb up the ingredient pyramid for that special occasion pie!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with a play on 'reach new heights' and 'ingredient pyramid,' but the explanation feels slightly forced and the ladder reference could be more integrated."}

candidates.text: Why did the chef bring a ladder to the kitchen? Because he heard there's no such thing as a "special occasion" when you're that tall!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'special occasion' and 'that tall,' but it feels a bit forced and lacks strong specificity to the cooking theme."}

candidates.text: Why did the chef bring a ladder to work? Because he wanted to reach new *highs* in his cooking recipes!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'highs,' but it's a bit generic and predictable for a humor competition."}

candidates.text: Why did the cook cross the kitchen? To get to the *other* side of the stove!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is a play on the classic 'Why did the chicken cross the road?' but with a cooking twist. It's somewhat surprising and specific to the topic, but the humor is mild and lacks originality."}
